core_mechanism: Coarse-graining of a high-dimensional system to a low-dimensional effective potential on a curved parameter (moduli) space, whose equilibria are the stationary (critical) points of that potential, together with a proof that these reduced stationarity conditions coincide with a spatially-integrated (averaged) form of the full fine-scale governing equations
brief_reason: The abstract reduces a higher-dimensional theory to an effective low-dimensional action, equips its parameter space with a special-Kaehler metric, characterizes vacua as conditions on that effective action, and proves those conditions are equivalent to an integrated version of the underlying full-scale equations.
